bootstrap.title: Remote Bootstrap
bootstrap.subtitle: Set up a remote machine to run ClawSquire and OpenClaw
bootstrap.autoDesc: Enter SSH connection info and we'll automatically install and configure everything on the remote machine.
bootstrap.autoSuccess: Setup complete! Connected to remote agent.
bootstrap.step1Desc: Run this script on the remote machine to install the agent binary. After running, it will print a URL and token for connection.
bootstrap.step2Desc: Use the target switcher in the top bar to connect to the remote agent using the URL and token from step 1.
bootstrap.notConnected: Not connected to a remote agent. Complete steps 1 and 2 first.
bootstrap.connectFirst: Connect to a remote agent first to detect the environment.
bootstrap.allReady: All dependencies are installed. Remote agent is ready!

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.safetyDescription: Only basic features are enabled. Advanced features require manual activation.

install.desc: Install OpenClaw to unlock all features — setup wizard, health checks, config management.
install.appResponsive: This may take 2-3 minutes. The app remains responsive.
install.npmRequiredDesc: OpenClaw requires Node.js (v18+) and npm. You can auto-install it or download manually.
install.nodeInstallHint: Downloading Node.js binary (~30MB). No admin rights needed.

llmGuide.title: Next Step: Connect an AI Model
llmGuide.desc: OpenClaw is installed! But it needs an AI model provider to work. Set one up now — it only takes 2 minutes.
llmGuide.reason1: OpenClaw uses AI models (like GPT, Claude, DeepSeek) to understand and respond to you
llmGuide.reason2: You need an API key from a provider to activate the AI
llmGuide.reason3: Free options are available (e.g. DeepSeek offers free credits)

ready.title: OpenClaw is Ready!
ready.desc: Your AI provider ({{provider}}) is configured. OpenClaw can now execute tasks for you!
ready.startDaemonHint: Start the Gateway to begin using your AI assistant.
ready.tryChatPlaceholder: Ask OpenClaw to do something... e.g. 'Summarize my recent emails'
ready.setupBotsHint: Optional: Set up Telegram/Discord bots for remote access →

webDashboard.title: OpenClaw Web Dashboard
webDashboard.desc: Open OpenClaw's built-in 12-tab control panel in your browser.

cli.title: OpenClaw CLI
cli.hint: Commands are passed to openclaw CLI. Example: status, config get gateway.auth.token, channels list

doctor.title: Health Check
doctor.description: Check your OpenClaw installation, configuration, and security.

search.title: Community Search
search.placeholder: Describe your issue, e.g. "node not found" or "gateway crash"
search.noResults: No matching issues found. Try different keywords.
search.cta: Search community for solutions to failed checks
search.subtitle: Search 11,000+ OpenClaw issues for solutions to common problems.
search.poweredBy: Powered by GitHub Search API — openclaw/openclaw

backup.title: Config Backups
backup.label: Label
backup.confirmRestore: Restore this backup? Current config will be backed up first.
backup.noBackups: No backups yet. Create your first backup to protect your config.
backup.noDifferences: No differences — config matches this backup.

telegram.name: Telegram Bot
telegram.description: Set up OpenClaw as a Telegram chat bot

whatsapp.name: WhatsApp
whatsapp.description: Connect OpenClaw to your WhatsApp via QR code

discord.name: Discord Bot
discord.description: Configure OpenClaw as a Discord bot

llm-provider.name: LLM Provider
llm-provider.description: Quickly connect an LLM provider (DeepSeek, OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama)

email-telegram.name: Email → Telegram
email-telegram.description: Monitor an email inbox and push summaries to Telegram

vps-headless.name: VPS Headless Deploy
vps-headless.description: Deploy OpenClaw on a headless VPS server

vps-bootstrap.name: Remote VPS Setup
vps-bootstrap.description: Manage and deploy OpenClaw on a remote VPS server

wizard.prereqNotInstalled: OpenClaw is not installed. Please install it first.
wizard.completeDesc: Your configuration has been saved successfully.

telegram.step1Desc: Open Telegram, search for @BotFather, and send /newbot. Follow the prompts to create a new bot. Copy the token it gives you.
telegram.step2Desc: Paste the bot token you received from @BotFather. It looks like 123456789:ABCdef...
telegram.step3Desc: Select which AI model provider your Telegram bot should use for replies.

whatsapp.step1Desc: OpenClaw connects to WhatsApp through WhatsApp Web. Run 'openclaw channels login --channel whatsapp' in your terminal, then scan the QR code with your phone.
whatsapp.completeDesc: WhatsApp is connected! You can now chat with OpenClaw directly in WhatsApp.

discord.step1Desc: Go to the Discord Developer Portal, create a new Application, then add a Bot to it. Copy the bot token.
discord.step2Desc: Paste the bot token from your Discord application settings.

llmProvider.step1Desc: Select the AI model provider you want to connect.
llmProvider.step2Desc: Sign up on the provider's website and copy your API key.
llmProvider.ollamaNote: Ollama runs locally. Make sure it's installed and running on your machine.
llmProvider.step3Desc: Pick which model to use by default. You can change this anytime.
llmProvider.noModels: No models found for this provider.
llmProvider.skipModelHint: You can skip this step and configure a model later.

emailTelegram.step1Desc: OpenClaw will periodically check your email inbox and send a summary of new messages to your Telegram bot. First, create a Telegram bot via @BotFather if you haven't already.
emailTelegram.step2Desc: Paste the bot token from @BotFather. Start a chat with your bot so it can send you messages.
emailTelegram.step3Desc: Enter the email address to monitor. OpenClaw uses IMAP to check for new messages.
emailTelegram.step4Desc: How often should OpenClaw check for new emails?
emailTelegram.completeDesc: OpenClaw will now check your email and push summaries to Telegram. You can manage this cron job from the Dashboard.

vpsHeadless.step1Desc: Daemon mode runs OpenClaw as a background service that starts automatically.
vpsHeadless.step2Desc: Set a secret token to secure remote access to your OpenClaw gateway.

wizard.apiKeyHint: Stored locally only. Never uploaded anywhere.
wizard.llmReadyDesc: AI provider configured. OpenClaw can now chat, answer questions, and run tasks for you — no bot setup needed!
wizard.optionalBotsHint: Want remote access? Set up Telegram or Discord bots from the Setup page.
wizard.tryChatDesc: Send a message to test your OpenClaw AI setup. (Requires the Gateway to be running.)
wizard.tryChatError: Could not reach the Gateway. Make sure the daemon is running (Dashboard → Start).

modelGuide.title: Choose your AI Model Provider
modelGuide.recommendation: Not sure which one? We recommend DeepSeek — it's free to start and works great.

deepseek.name: DeepSeek
deepseek.features: Great for Chinese, simple signup (~2 min)

openai.name: OpenAI
openai.features: Most popular globally, requires payment (~5 min)

ollama.name: Ollama
ollama.features: Completely free! Requires a good GPU

steps.keyLocalOnly: Your key is stored locally only and never uploaded anywhere.

settings.title: Settings
settings.customHint: Custom permissions: fine-tune via Config page or openclaw CLI.
settings.uninstallDescription: Stop daemon, remove system service, and uninstall OpenClaw. Your config can be backed up first.

uninstall.confirmDesc: This will stop the OpenClaw daemon and uninstall the package.
uninstall.autoBackupNote: A backup will be created automatically before uninstalling.
uninstall.appResponsive: Uninstalling OpenClaw... The app remains responsive.

settings.safetyApplying: Applying safety preset to OpenClaw config...
settings.safetyLocalOnly: OpenClaw not installed — preset saved locally and will be applied after installation.

conservative.name: Conservative
conservative.description: Only chat and config management. Recommended for beginners.

standard.name: Standard
standard.description: Adds browser control, file access, and skills. For users with some technical background.

full.name: Full Access
full.description: All features enabled. For developers and advanced users.

welcome.title: Welcome to ClawSquire
welcome.subtitle: Your OpenClaw companion app
welcome.openclawNotFound: OpenClaw is not installed on this system.
welcome.installGuide: Install OpenClaw first, then come back here.
welcome.safetyNotice: ClawSquire starts with the safest settings by default. You can always enable more features later.

gateway.title: What is a Gateway?
gateway.explanation: The Gateway is OpenClaw's "brain" — it runs on your computer, receives your messages, calls AI models to process them, and sends back results.
gateway.tip: Most users should choose "Run on this computer".

node.title: What is a Node?
node.explanation: A Node is any device (phone, computer) that can act as OpenClaw's "hands and feet" — taking photos, reading contacts, getting location, etc.
node.tip: Your phone can be a Node via the official iOS/Android app.

agent.title: What is an Agent?
agent.explanation: The Agent is the AI assistant itself. It can help you with tasks like answering questions, browsing the web, or writing code.
agent.tip: The agent's abilities depend on which permissions you grant.

llmProvider.title: What is an LLM Provider?
llmProvider.explanation: An LLM Provider is the company that provides the AI "brain" (like DeepSeek, OpenAI, Anthropic). You need to register with one to get an API Key.
llmProvider.tip: Think of it like a restaurant membership — you need a card to order food.

apiKey.title: What is an API Key?
apiKey.explanation: An API Key is a secret password that lets OpenClaw use an AI service. Each provider gives you one when you register.
apiKey.tip: Keep it private! Never share your API Key with anyone. ClawSquire stores it locally only.

daemon.title: What is a Daemon?
daemon.explanation: A Daemon is a background service that keeps OpenClaw running all the time, even when you're not actively using it.
daemon.tip: Like a night watchman — always on duty, even while you sleep.

sandbox.title: What is Sandbox Mode?
sandbox.explanation: Sandbox mode limits what the AI can do on your computer. It prevents it from accessing files outside a safe area or running dangerous commands.
sandbox.tip: We strongly recommend keeping Sandbox ON, especially when you're new.

channel.title: What is a Channel?
channel.explanation: A Channel is the way you communicate with your AI assistant — like Telegram, WhatsApp, or Discord.
channel.tip: You can set up multiple channels, but one is enough to get started.

skill.title: What is a Skill?
skill.explanation: A Skill gives the AI new abilities — like browsing the web, writing code, or managing files. Skills can be installed and removed.
skill.tip: Start without extra skills. You can always add them later.

workspace.title: What is a Workspace?
workspace.explanation: The Workspace is the folder where the AI mainly operates. It reads and writes files here. Think of it as the AI's "desk".
workspace.tip: Choose a dedicated folder for this. Don't use your home directory.

config.title: What is a Config?
config.explanation: The Config (configuration) is a file that stores all your settings — which AI provider to use, which channels, permissions, etc.
config.tip: ClawSquire manages this file for you. You don't need to edit it manually.

token.title: What is a Token/Auth?
token.explanation: A Token is a security credential that prevents unauthorized people from controlling your AI. It's like a lock on your front door.
token.tip: Always set a gateway token. ClawSquire enables this by default.

gatewayWontStart.question: Gateway won't start?
gatewayWontStart.answer: Try these steps: 1) Check if OpenClaw is properly installed (run Health Check). 2) Check if another process is using port 18789. 3) Restart the daemon from Dashboard.

whereToGetApiKey.question: Where do I get an API Key?
whereToGetApiKey.answer: Go to Setup → LLM Provider. We'll guide you through creating an account and getting your API Key step by step.

whatIsSandbox.question: What is Sandbox mode? Should I enable it?
whatIsSandbox.answer: Sandbox mode limits what the AI can do on your computer. We strongly recommend keeping it ON, especially as a beginner. It prevents the AI from accessing sensitive files or running dangerous commands.

howToCreateBot.question: How do I create a Telegram Bot?
howToCreateBot.answer: Go to Setup → Telegram Group Assistant. The wizard will guide you through creating a bot with @BotFather and connecting it to OpenClaw.

doctorCheckFailed.question: Health Check failed — what do I do?
doctorCheckFailed.answer: Click on the failed check to see details. Most issues have a 'Fix' button that will resolve them automatically. If not, the help text explains what to do manually.

isMyDataSafe.question: Is my data safe?
isMyDataSafe.answer: Yes. ClawSquire runs entirely on your computer. Your API keys, config files, and all data stay local. Nothing is uploaded to any server. ClawSquire is open source (MIT license) — you can verify this yourself.

canIUndo.question: Can I undo changes?
canIUndo.answer: Yes! Use Backups to save snapshots of your configuration. You can compare changes and restore any previous version at any time.

whatIfSomethingBreaks.question: What if something breaks?
whatIfSomethingBreaks.answer: Don't worry! ClawSquire automatically creates a backup before making changes. You can always restore from Backups. If OpenClaw itself has issues, run Health Check for diagnosis.

helpPanel.title: Help
helpPanel.footer: Press Esc to close · Cmd+? for quick access

config.title: Configuration
config.notFound: Config file not found. Is OpenClaw installed?
config.secretsMasked: Secrets (API keys, tokens) are partially masked for security.

feedback.title: Send Feedback
feedback.descriptionPlaceholder: Tell us what happened or what you'd like to see...
feedback.opensGithub: This will open a pre-filled GitHub issue in your browser.

remote.title: Remote Install
remote.subtitle: Generate a one-line command to install OpenClaw on your VPS
remote.securityNote: API keys are NOT included in the install command. You will configure them securely after installation completes on the server.
remote.providerHint: Pre-select a provider. You'll enter the API key on the server after install.
remote.channelHint: Pre-select a messaging channel. Token is configured after install.
remote.postInstall: After installation, run these on the server:

ssh.title: SSH Connection
ssh.description: Connect to your VPS and run commands directly from ClawSquire.
ssh.keyPathHint: Path to your SSH private key file (e.g. ~/.ssh/id_ed25519)

vps.addDesc: Connect to a remote VPS. Test the connection first, then save.
vps.passwordSessionOnly: Password is kept in memory only — never saved to disk.
vps.setupDesc: Use the Bootstrap wizard to install clawsquire-serve and OpenClaw on this remote machine.
vps.alreadySetup: OpenClaw is already installed on this instance. Use Connect to manage it via protocol.
vps.emptyDesc: Add an instance to deploy and manage OpenClaw remotely via protocol.

imap.title: Email Setup
imap.subtitle: Configure IMAP access so OpenClaw can read your emails.
imap.step1Desc: We'll auto-detect the IMAP server settings for your email provider.
imap.providerHint: Gmail, Outlook, QQ Mail, 163, Yahoo, iCloud and many more are auto-detected.
imap.step2Desc: Verify or edit the IMAP server settings. Most providers use port 993 with TLS.
imap.step3Desc: Enter the password or app-specific password for this email account.
imap.appPasswordHint: For Gmail/Outlook: use an App Password, not your regular password. Go to your account security settings to generate one.
imap.doneDesc: IMAP credentials saved to OpenClaw config. The Email→Telegram cron job can now access your inbox.

channels.title: Channels
channels.description: Manage communication channels that connect OpenClaw to your messaging apps.
channels.emptyDesc: Add a channel to let OpenClaw communicate through Telegram, Discord, and more.
channels.noTokenGeneric: This channel requires manual setup via terminal.

setup.telegram: Create a bot via @BotFather on Telegram and paste the token below.
setup.discord: Create an app on the Discord Developer Portal. Copy the Bot Token.
setup.whatsapp: WhatsApp requires QR code pairing via terminal. Run: openclaw channels login --channel whatsapp
setup.slack: Create a Slack app and copy the Bot User OAuth Token.
setup.webchat: WebChat is built into the OpenClaw gateway. No extra setup needed.

noToken.whatsapp: WhatsApp uses QR code pairing. Run this in your terminal: openclaw channels login --channel whatsapp
noToken.webchat: WebChat is built into the gateway. Start the gateway and visit the WebChat URL.

sources.title: Input Sources
sources.description: Configure data sources that OpenClaw can read from — email inboxes, RSS feeds, and more.
sources.tipDesc: Once a source is configured, create an Automation to periodically check it and send summaries to your channels.

imap.name: Email (IMAP)
imap.desc: Read emails from any IMAP-compatible inbox

rss.name: RSS Feeds
rss.desc: Monitor blog posts, news, and website updates

automations.title: Automations
automations.description: Schedule recurring tasks — the AI agent runs them automatically and sends results to your channels.
automations.emptyDesc: Create an automation to have OpenClaw check your email, send reminders, or run custom tasks on a schedule.
automations.noChannelsHint: Add a channel first (Channels page) so automations can send results.
automations.choosePreset: Choose a template to get started, or create a fully custom automation.
automations.messagePlaceholder: What should the AI agent do each time this runs?

email-summary.name: Email Summary
email-summary.defaultMessage: Check for new unread emails. Summarize any important ones briefly. If nothing new, stay silent.

scheduled-reminder.name: Scheduled Reminder
scheduled-reminder.defaultMessage: Give me a brief summary of today's weather and any important news.

custom.name: Custom
custom.desc: Define your own task from scratch
